title: mentzelia gracilenta var. pectinata
intro: Mentzelia gracilenta var. pectinata, also known as the sandstickyskipper or pectinate stickleaf, is a rare, flowering plant in the evening primrose family native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. The medicinal properties of Mentzelia gracilenta var. pectinata have been reported to provide health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, and it can be used as a tea or applied topically as a poultice or salve to harness its medicinal properties.
